expectations.text: Message body (excluding meta-line) is under 1000 characters
expectations.evidence: Message body (before ---) is approximately 380 characters — under 1000.

expectations.text: Contains at least one Voss label
expectations.evidence: 'It sounds like we're working in pretty similar territory' — matches 'It sounds like' pattern.

expectations.text: Does not pitch NexusPoint services as if Steven is a prospective client
expectations.evidence: No service pitch to Steven as buyer. Describes Aleem's work area but doesn't offer to sell him anything.

expectations.text: Does not ask Steven to 'hop on a call' as a prospective buyer
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a quick call to chat about what you're building' — framed as peer/collaboration, not as a sales call to a buyer.

expectations.text: Frames the conversation as peer-to-peer / partnership territory — e.g., asks about his build-side setup, overflow work, specialist partners, or referral dynamics
expectations.evidence: 'potential to collaborate - whether that's referrals, subcontracting, or just staying in each other's network' — peer framing present.

expectations.text: Meta-line (Phase or Tactic) flags that this is a peer/referral-partner track, not a direct-client track
expectations.evidence: 'Phase: Qualify' with no peer-track qualifier. Tactic mentions 'peer-framed' but the Phase declaration doesn't flag the peer track explicitly. The skill expects 'Qualify (peer track)' or equivalent.

expectations.text: Output includes Phase: and Tactic: meta-line

expectations.evidence: No em dashes. Plain hyphens used.

expectations.text: Contains a calibrated question that surfaces the nature of his build-side setup
expectations.evidence: 'Would you be open to a quick call to chat about what you're building and where our work might overlap?' — this is a yes/no call ask, not a calibrated question that surfaces the build-side structure. It doesn't ask whether he handles builds himself vs. using specialists.

eval_feedback.overall: Closer than the other without-skill outputs — natural peer framing is accessible without the skill. The two failures (no explicit peer-track phase label, and no build-side calibrated question) are the specific things the skill adds.
